verify.rule: cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value, and verify must include a mass/budget check.

verify.value: good
verify.rule: [power_signal_separation] good: Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels or are spaced/crossed at right angles, with the separation visible in routing geometry. | bad: Power and signal wires share one tight bundle along the whole route with no separation or shielding note.

verify.value: good
verify.rule: [return_path_and_grounding_intent] good: Each electronics module states its grounding scheme (star point, chassis bond, isolated) and signal returns route alongside their signals. | bad: Grounding is unmentioned; returns are implied through structure with no bond feature or note.

verify.value: good
verify.rule: [board_mounting_and_clearance] good: PCBs mount on standoffs with keep-out clearance to walls and moving parts, connector edges face access openings, and mounting holes match standard board patterns. | bad: Boards are floated or glued in cavities with no standoffs, clearance, or connector orientation logic.

verify.value: good
verify.rule: Each required evidence_feature must appear in part_tree/geometry_ops.

verify.value: good
verify.rule: Name exact neighboring subsystem labels when they mate, and choose connection_type + DOF + clearance_mm + required_feature so the neighbor can declare the same joint family back.

verify.value: good
verify.rule: Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent.

risk.desc: Potential thermal management issues if heat sink is not properly aligned with battery module.
risk.mit: Ensure proper alignment and thermal paste application during assembly.

risk.desc: Risk of electrical interference if power and signal lines are not properly separated.
risk.mit: Implement routing geometry that ensures separation of power and signal lines.

risk.desc: Risk of improper grounding if grounding scheme is not clearly defined.
risk.mit: Define grounding scheme in documentation and ensure it is implemented in design.

risk.desc: Risk of assembly errors if mounting holes and standoffs are not properly aligned.
risk.mit: Ensure alignment of mounting holes and standoffs during design and assembly.

risk.desc: Risk of service access issues if tool-less lid is not properly designed.
risk.mit: Ensure tool-less lid design allows for easy access during maintenance.

risk.desc: Potential for actuator socket misalignment due to press fit clearance.
risk.mit: Use a press fit with a clearance of 0.2mm to ensure proper alignment.

risk.desc: Risk of thimble socket misalignment due to snap fit clearance.
risk.mit: Ensure snap fit clearance is 0.5mm to allow for proper alignment.

risk.desc: Wrist band interface may not align properly due to band clamp clearance.
risk.mit: Set band clamp clearance to 1.0mm to ensure proper alignment.

risk.desc: Service access point may be difficult to reach due to design complexity.
risk.mit: Ensure service access point is easily accessible by placing it in a visible location.

risk.desc: Sensor pocket may interfere with finger movement due to placement.
risk.mit: Position sensor pocket away from critical motion paths to avoid interference.

physics_paths.description: Finger and wrist wiring uses service loops, strain relief at every flex boundary, and a stated bend radius sized for repeated flexion cycles.

Per-finger linkage cartridges are removable by single pin or visible hinge access.
